judgments.rationale: Selected bet bet-a is the most robust live alternative: it leads bet-b on continuity, implementation, and accountability, and it leads bet-c on continuity and implementation. The ranking is stable rather than a near tie, the comparison field is complete with three credible live alternatives, and there are no fog dependencies or hard stops. Evidence against is only weak and generated for all alternatives, while the identified limited failure mode is partially tested rather than critically untested.

Selected bet bet-a is the most robust live alternative: it leads bet-b on all three shared criteria and has a clear weighted advantage over bet-c despite bet-c's accountability lead. All live alternatives have the same weak generated-hypothesis evidence against, no fog dependencies, and the same limited partially tested failure mode, so these do not destabilize the ordering. No hard stop applies, and the field contains three credible live alternatives with complete common criterion coverage.

All three alternatives are live and share complete scores across the same three criteria, with no unresolved hard stop. bet-a is the selected bet and is most robust: it leads bet-b on every criterion and has the strongest weighted profile, driven especially by continuity. bet-b ranks second, ahead of bet-c on continuity and implementation; bet-c's accountability advantage does not offset its lower continuity and implementation. The weak generated implementation concern and limited, partially tested failure mode apply equally to every alternative, so they do not destabilize the ordering. There are no fog dependencies, no near-tie or weak-field flag, and the selection has grounded support.

All three alternatives are live and grounded, with no hard stop or incomplete comparison field. bet-b is most robust because it has only weak generated evidence against and strong continuity/implementation scores. bet-c likewise has only weak generated evidence against, though its lower continuity and implementation scores place it behind bet-b. The selected bet-a ranks last despite strong nominal scores because it uniquely carries material, traced student-observation evidence against implementation. That material evidence prevents a High rating for the selected position.

Selected bet bet-a is not the most robust position: it carries material, traced student-observation evidence against implementation, while bet-b has only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against and otherwise stronger weighted continuity and implementation support than bet-c. bet-a retains stronger scored support than bet-b, but the material adverse evidence prevents a High assessment and makes bet-b the more robust live alternative. All three alternatives are live, grounded, and comparably assessed; no hard stop applies.

All three alternatives are live and have a complete common criterion field, so no hard stop applies. bet-b is most robust: it leads bet-c on the more heavily weighted continuity and implementation criteria, with only weak generated evidence against and the same limited, partially tested failure exposure. bet-c follows, with stronger accountability but weaker continuity and implementation. Selected bet-a has the strongest raw criterion scores, but its material, traced student-observation evidence against implementation is materially stronger than the weak generated concerns attached to the other live options. That prevents a High judgment and places the selected position behind the less-contraindicated alternatives.

Selected bet bet-a is least robust because it has decisive evidence against continuity from a direct client reply. That sourced contradiction is dominant and caps the position at Low. Bet-b robustly outranks bet-c on all three shared criterion scores; both otherwise have the same weak generated implementation concern and the same limited, partially tested failure mode.

Selected bet: bet-a. All three alternatives are live and the common comparison field is complete. Bet-a has a decisive, directly sourced client-reply contradiction on continuity; this dominant adverse evidence makes it least robust and caps the selected position at Low. Bet-b is most robust on the supplied criterion scores without decisive contrary evidence. Bet-c ranks next: it is stronger than bet-b on implementation and accountability but materially weaker on the highest-weight continuity criterion. All candidates share only weak generated implementation concerns and a limited partially tested failure mode.

bet-a is the selected bet but has decisive, directly sourced evidence against it on continuity, so its robustness cannot exceed Low despite its higher criterion scores. bet-b and bet-c are live, grounded alternatives with only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against and the same limited partially tested failure mode; bet-b ranks ahead of bet-c on the supplied criterion scores. No hard stop is marked unresolved.

bet-a is the most robust live alternative on the supplied common criteria, leading bet-b and bet-c overall, with no stronger evidence against than the others. bet-b is second: it has no fog dependency but is lower than bet-a on continuity and accountability. bet-c is least robust because its accountability advantage does not offset lower continuity and implementation. Moderate rather than High because bet-a retains a materially influential partial fog dependency (0.7) and all alternatives retain a limited, only partially tested failure mode.

Selected bet: bet-a. It is the most robust live alternative on the shared weighted comparison, leading bet-b on continuity and accountability while tying it on implementation; bet-c trails on the more heavily weighted continuity and implementation criteria despite stronger accountability. All three are credible live alternatives with populated common criterion fields and support traces. Confidence cannot be High because bet-a has a materially influential partial fog dependency (influence 0.7), while its failure mode is only partially tested. The weak generated-hypothesis evidence against implementation is shared across all alternatives and is not a sourced contradiction.

Selected bet: bet-a. It is most robust on the shared comparison field, leading continuity and accountability while tying bet-b on implementation; this ordering is not a near tie on the supplied field. All live alternatives carry the same weak, generated-hypothesis evidence against implementation and the same limited, partially tested failure mode, so those factors do not reverse the ranking. However, bet-a has a partial fog dependency with 0.7 influence. Although noncritical and not contradicted, that unresolved fog prevents a High rating. Bet-b is next due to stronger continuity and implementation than bet-c; bet-c trails on those dimensions despite its accountability lead.

bet-a has the strongest stated criterion scores, but its critical, influence-1 fog dependency is unaddressed. That materially unresolved exposure makes the selected position less robust than the fog-free live alternatives and prevents a rating above Low. bet-b is most robust because it leads bet-c on continuity and implementation with no disclosed fog dependency; bet-c follows, with stronger accountability but lower continuity and implementation. All three have only weak generated evidence against and similarly limited, partially tested failure modes.

Selected bet: bet-a. All three alternatives are live and share complete criterion coverage, so scoring is possible. Bet-a has the strongest nominal weighted criterion profile, but its critical, influence-1 fog dependency is wholly unaddressed. This materially unresolved exposure makes its apparent advantage non-robust and places it behind the otherwise similarly evidenced alternatives. Bet-b is most robust on the available record due to its stronger implementation score and no fog dependencies; bet-c follows with lower continuity and implementation. The selected position cannot exceed Low because its critical fog remains unresolved.

Selected bet bet-a is Low robustness: although it has the strongest scored profile, it depends on an unaddressed critical fog item with full influence, materially limiting confidence in its position. bet-b is most robust because it has a comparatively strong score profile with no fog dependency; its only adverse evidence is weak and generated, and its limited failure mode is only partially tested. bet-c ranks ahead of bet-a because it likewise has no fog dependency, despite weaker continuity and implementation scores. All three are live and have the same complete comparison fields, so no hard stop applies.

Selected bet: bet-a. All three alternatives are live and have complete criterion fields. Bet-a has the strongest stated criterion scores, but it carries a catastrophic untested failure mode, a critical unresolved exposure that caps the selected position at Low and makes it less robust than the alternatives without that exposure. Bet-b ranks ahead of bet-c on the weighted continuity and implementation field; both have only weak generated evidence against and the same limited partially tested failure mode.

Selected bet bet-a is least robust because it alone carries a catastrophic, untested failure mode; this critical unresolved exposure caps the selected position at Low. bet-b ranks first on the supplied criterion scores among alternatives without that exposure, followed by bet-c. All three are live and have common comparison fields, so no hard stop applies.

Selected bet bet-a is Low robustness because it has a catastrophic untested failure mode, a critical unresolved failure exposure. bet-b is most robust: it has no fog dependencies, only a limited partially tested failure mode, and stronger continuity and implementation than bet-c. bet-c follows, with the same limited partially tested failure exposure but weaker continuity and implementation, despite stronger accountability. bet-a ranks last despite its higher criterion scores because its catastrophic failure mode remains untested.

All three alternatives are live and have complete scores across the common criteria, so no hard stop applies. bet-a is the selected bet and ranks first on the weighted criterion scores, narrowly ahead of bet-b; bet-c is materially lower overall despite stronger accountability. Each option has the same weak, generated (unsourced) implementation concern, no fog dependencies, and the same limited partially tested failure mode, so these do not differentiate the ranking. The very narrow bet-a/bet-b margin prevents a High robustness judgment.

bet-a is the selected bet and is marginally most robust on the common weighted criterion profile, leading bet-b on continuity, implementation, and accountability. bet-b is a very close second with the same weak generated counterevidence, no fog dependencies, and the same partially tested limited failure mode; this narrow separation limits ranking stability and precludes High. bet-c ranks third because its continuity score is materially lower, outweighing its stronger accountability score under the stated weights. No hard stop is indicated: the comparison set is complete and contains three credible live alternatives with common comparison fields.

bet-a is the selected bet and is marginally strongest across all three common criteria versus bet-b, while bet-c trails materially on continuity and implementation despite stronger accountability. All three live alternatives have the same weak generated evidence against and the same limited, partially tested failure mode, with no fog dependencies, so these factors do not differentiate the ordering. The bet-a/b margin is narrow, preventing a High robustness label.

bet-a is the most robust live alternative on the supplied common field, led by materially stronger continuity and accountability while remaining competitive on implementation. bet-b ranks second: its implementation lead does not offset lower continuity and accountability under the stated weights. bet-c ranks third because it trails bet-a across continuity and implementation and has no accountability advantage. All three share the same weak generated evidence against, no fog dependencies, and the same limited partially tested failure mode, so these factors do not differentiate the ranking. The weak-field flag prevents a High rating.

bet-a is most robust on the common comparison field: it leads bet-b on the highest-weight continuity criterion and on accountability, while conceding only a smaller implementation advantage. bet-c is clearly behind on continuity and implementation. All three live alternatives have the same weak generated evidence-against item, no fog dependencies, and the same limited partially tested failure mode, so these do not differentiate the ranking. The weak-field flag prevents a High judgment.

bet-a is the most robust live option on the supplied common criteria, leading bet-b on continuity and accountability while remaining close on implementation. bet-b is second, with stronger implementation but lower continuity and accountability; bet-c trails on continuity. All three share the same weak generated evidence against and limited, partially tested failure mode, with no fog dependencies. The weak-field flag prevents a High rating.

Selected bet bet-a is least robust: both bet-b and bet-c match or exceed it on every supplied criterion, while bet-a alone carries a critical, high-influence unaddressed fog dependency and a material untested failure mode. Those unresolved exposures cap the selected position at Low. bet-b ranks ahead of bet-c on the supplied continuity and implementation scores; both have the same weak generated-hypothesis evidence against and no listed fog dependencies.

All three alternatives are live and provide scores for each common criterion, so no hard-stop NoScore condition is triggered. bet-b is most robust because it exceeds bet-c on all three listed criterion scores with otherwise matching weak generated evidence-against and limited partially tested failure exposure. bet-c is next: it has no fog dependencies and no material untested failure mode, unlike the selected bet. bet-a is least robust despite being selected because it depends on an unaddressed critical fog with high influence (0.8) and retains a material untested failure mode. Those unresolved exposures cap the selected bet at Low.

bet-b is most robust: it has the strongest scored position across all three common criteria and only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against it, with no fog dependencies and no material untested failure mode. bet-c is next: its continuity score is below bet-b's, though its implementation and accountability scores are strong; its adverse evidence and limited partially tested failure exposure match bet-b's. It trails both alternatives on implementation and accountability, depends on an unaddressed critical fog item with high influence, and retains a material untested failure mode. That critical unresolved fog and failure exposure cap the selected position at Low.

Selected bet: bet-a. All three alternatives are live and have a complete common comparison field, with no stated hard stop. bet-a has strong criterion scores, but it carries two evidence-against items and a partially unresolved fog dependency with 0.5 influence; this makes its position less robust than the alternatives with only one weak generated-hypothesis concern and no fog dependency. bet-b is most robust on the supplied matrix because it has the strongest continuity/implementation balance among the no-fog options. bet-c follows, with weaker continuity but no fog exposure. Selected bet: bet-a. bet-b is most robust because it has only one weak generated evidence-against item and no fog dependency. bet-a ranks second: although its criterion scores are stronger than bet-b’s, it carries two weak evidence-against items plus a partially unresolved fog dependency with 0.5 influence. bet-c ranks last on the supplied comparison because its continuity and implementation scores are materially lower; it has no offsetting robustness advantage over bet-b.
